skill.name: brainstorming
skill.description: Guides early design work through structured questions, explicit assumptions, alternative approaches, and documented decisions before implementation begins.
skill.summary: Turn vague ideas into validated designs before building.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: AI review found no evidence of weak cryptography, command execution, environment access, network reconnaissance, or malicious prompt injection in SKILL.md. Static findings are false positives from natural-language Markdown, except the skill has expected low-risk filesystem exposure because it asks the assistant to review project context and persist design documentation.

low_findings.title: Project File Review and Documentation Writes
low_findings.description: The skill instructs the assistant to review available project files, documentation, plans, and prior decisions, then later persist the final design according to the project workflow. This is expected for a design-planning skill, but users should avoid running it on repositories containing sensitive material unless that project context is intended for the assistant.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines clearly instruct project context review and durable documentation. The risk is low because there is no network transfer, secret access, or executable code.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer False Positives Reviewed
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptography detections are ordinary brainstorming and design-process text, not cryptographic code. The reported external command at line 230 is a backticked skill name, and the reported environment access at line 52 is ordinary prose about implicit constraints.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed file is Markdown instructions with no shell syntax, imports, cryptographic APIs, or environment variable references. The exact flagged lines contain prose and a skill identifier.

content.value_statement: Early implementation often fails because assumptions and requirements are unclear. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through structured discovery, tradeoff review, and decision documentation.
actual_capabilities: Clarifies goals, users, constraints, success criteria, and non-goals before design work starts.
Requires explicit coverage of performance, scale, security, reliability, and maintenance assumptions.
Creates an understanding summary and waits for user confirmation before proposing designs.
Compares two or three design approaches with tradeoffs for complexity, extensibility, risk, and maintenance.
Maintains a decision log that can be included in final design documentation.
Prepares an implementation handoff only after the design is validated and documented.
limitations: It does not implement code while brainstorming mode is active.
It depends on user confirmation before moving from understanding to design.
It cannot validate facts that are not present in the project or provided by the user.
It may take longer than direct implementation because it enforces staged clarification.

use_cases.title: Shape a New Product Feature
use_cases.description: Clarify user needs, constraints, non-goals, and success criteria before choosing a feature design.

use_cases.title: Plan an Architecture Change
use_cases.description: Compare viable technical approaches and record decisions before changing shared systems.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Safe Implementation Brief
use_cases.description: Turn an unclear request into confirmed requirements, documented assumptions, and a staged implementation plan.

prompt_templates.title: Start Requirements Discovery
prompt_templates.scenario: Begin from a rough idea with little structure.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the brainstorming skill to help me clarify this idea before implementation: [describe the idea]. Ask one question at a time.

prompt_templates.title: Clarify Non-Functional Requirements
prompt_templates.scenario: Add operational constraints before design options are proposed.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the brainstorming skill to identify performance, scale, security, reliability, and maintenance assumptions for this project: [project context].

prompt_templates.title: Compare Design Approaches
prompt_templates.scenario: Evaluate options after the goal and constraints are confirmed.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the brainstorming skill to propose two or three approaches for [confirmed goal]. Include tradeoffs and recommend one option.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Design Handoff
prompt_templates.scenario: Finalize a documented plan before implementation begins.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the brainstorming skill to produce a final design handoff for [confirmed design]. Include the understanding summary, assumptions, decision log, risks, and implementation readiness.

output_examples.input: I want to add team workspaces to our app.
output: A short understanding summary covering the workspace goal, target users, constraints, and non-goals.
A focused question about ownership, permissions, or billing boundaries.
A clear pause asking for confirmation before design options are created.

output_examples.input: We need to redesign our notification system.
output: Two or three architecture options with tradeoffs for scale, reliability, complexity, and maintenance.
A recommended approach with risks and assumptions called out.
A decision log that explains why the selected direction was chosen.

output_examples.input: The design is approved. Prepare the handoff.
output: A final design document structure with context, assumptions, decisions, risks, and testing strategy.
A readiness check before implementation planning starts.

best_practices: Provide current project context and known constraints before asking for design help.
Confirm or correct the understanding summary before requesting design options.
Keep the decision log with the project documentation after the design is approved.
anti_patterns: Using the skill to skip discovery and jump directly into code changes.
Treating proposed assumptions as facts without user confirmation.
Asking for a final architecture before success criteria and non-goals are clear.

faq.question: Does this skill write code?
faq.answer: No. It explicitly blocks implementation until the design is validated and documented.

faq.question: When should I use it?
faq.answer: Use it before feature work, architecture changes, behavior changes, or other constructive work with unclear requirements.

faq.question: Why does it ask one question at a time?
faq.answer: Single questions reduce confusion and help the user confirm each important requirement.

faq.question: Can it help with security requirements?
faq.answer: Yes. It requires explicit discussion of security and privacy constraints during non-functional requirement discovery.

faq.question: What does the decision log include?
faq.answer: It records each decision, alternatives considered, and the reason the selected option was chosen.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The audit found no malicious behavior, but users should be aware it may review project files and write design documentation.
